    [Guide] Simple Plugin Example - HB 3.x

    1. speshulk926 Community Developer

      Joined:
      Dec 9, 2014
      Messages:
      911
      Likes Received:

      Trophy Points:
      18
      There are a lot of questions on how to set up plugins (correctly). I wanted to share this information that I've gotten and will eventually add some more onto this thread to show more advanced Plugin examples. To start this off we will work with the most basic plugin with no settings.

      What you need:
      • Visual Studio 2015
      • Honorbuddy 3.x

      Skip Ahead
      If you have already done this part and just want to skip ahead, use the following links:
      Simple Plugin; No Settings
      Simple Plugin; With Settings
      Simple Plugin; Coroutine Logic

      Initial Setup
      No matter if you are doing an advanced plugin or a standard plugin, the setup is pretty much the same.

      Open Visual Studio 2015
      New > Project > Class Library > Enter Useful Name


      Right click References on the right and click on Add Reference...


      Point to your Honorbuddy.exe file


      Also add PresentationCore from Assemblies > Framework tab on the left (this is for Logging colors)


      Double-click Properties on the right hand side to pull up the Project Properties.


      • Change the Target Framework to the latest framework HB is using at the time. As of this date, that is 4.6.1.


        Go to Build tab and then change Platform target to x86 (x86 = 32 bit, which will get rid of the warning message, but really isn't necessary)


      Rename Class1 to YourPluginName (or whatever useful name you can come up with)


      Now you need to tell Honorbuddy what the main class is for your plugin. You do this by inheriting HBPlugin.


      As you can see this causes an error. It doesn't know where HBPlugin is located. If you press "Ctrl + . " (Control + Period), a menu will load and ask you if you want to add a using Styx.Plugins. Most of the time, it gets this correct and can proceed. Be cautious when using this though if there's more than 1 suggestion. You can use this shortcut all over if there's code you know exists somewhere but cannot remember the exact location of it.


      After adding the using reference, you will see we now have a new error. This error is there because we have successfully inherited the HBPlugin class, but the HBPlugin class has some Abstract members that must be initialized first before we will compile correctly.


      We will do the exact same thing as before, but this time put the cursor on "TheBestPlugin" text and hit Ctrl + Period. This will implement the Abstract members that it requires.


      At this point, we have code that will successfully compile, but there's no code in it. We will now add some code into it and see what we can do.


      We have 4 total required items that were added:
      • Author - Just who the plugin was created by.
      • Name - Your plugin name
      • Version - The version of your plugin
      • Pulse - This is the method call where your code will go. Pulse is executed multiple times per second.
      After we update Author, Name and Version, it'll look like this (I also shrunk the code down a bit to make it easier to read):


      Those are the required items, but there are also other optional ones that you will likely want.
      • OnEnable method - This executes when the Plugin is enabled
      • OnDisable method - This executes when the Plugin is disabled
      • ButtonText property - This is the string of text that will appear on the "Settings button".
      • WantButton property - This is what tells HB if you want a "Settings button".
      • OnButtonPress method - This is what will happen when someone clicks on the "Settings button"

      I will focus on the settings in a different part, but for now, we can just add OnEnable and OnDisable so we execute something when the plugin is enabled or disabled. That part is optional; only if you want/need to.
      You can add these 2 methods into your code.
      PHP:
      public override void OnEnable()
      {
                base.OnEnable();

      }
      public       override void OnDisable()
      {
                base.OnDisable();
      }

      Our initial setup is complete! You may now use the below instructions to do something specified that you need to do.
       

      Attached Files:

    2. speshulk926 Community Developer

      Joined:
      Dec 9, 2014
      Messages:
      911
      Likes Received:
      17
      Trophy Points:
      18
      Simple Plugin; No Settings

      So now we have everything implemented other than the code we need to execute. Plugins have a wide variety of different things they can do. For this example, all we will do is keep track of how much HP I currently have and log when it goes below 50% in combat. I know it's kind of useless, but it's all I could think of to do that was fairly easy.


      First, let's start off by adding a "Me" read-only property so we can get our Health. You will see this a lot in Combat Routines and Plugins. It makes things simple to read.
      Code:
      public Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.LocalPlayer Me { get { return Styx.StyxWoW.Me; } }



      One thing I always do is make a method call for Logging. This enforces a standardization of logging while keeping things really simple. I usually do an infoLog, combatLog (if there's combat in your plugin), and a debugLog. I will only be doing infoLog and debugLog for this.




      I will add the code for making sure I am valid, am in game, and in combat and then write the log when I drop below 50%.
      PHP:
      using Styx.Common;
      using Styx.Plugins;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TheBestPlugin : HBPlugin
                {
              public       override string Author { get { return "SpeshulK926"; } }
              public       override string Name { get { return "The Best Plugin!"; } }
              public       override Version Version { get { return new Version(1, 0); } }
              public       Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.LocalPlayer Me { get { return Styx.StyxWoW.Me; } }
              public       override void Pulse()
              {
                        // if any of this is true, then we won't run the code  
                        if (!Styx.StyxWoW.IsInGame || !Styx.StyxWoW.IsInWorld || Me == null || !Me.IsValid || !Me.IsAlive || Me.Mounted || !Me.Combat)
                  {
                      return;
                  }

                  if (      Me.HealthPercent < 50)
                  {
                            infoLog("Current Health dropped below 50%! Health currently at {0}", Me.HealthPercent);
                  }
              }
              public       override void OnEnable()
              {
                        base.OnEnable();
                        infoLog("Enabled!");
              }
              public       override void OnDisable()
              {
                        base.OnDisable();
                        infoLog("Disabled!");
              }
              public static       void infoLog(string message, params object[] args)
              {
                        Logging.Write(System.Windows.Media.Colors.White, "[TBP] " + message, args);
              }
              public static       void debugLog(string message, params object[] args)
              {
                        Logging.WriteQuiet("[TBP] " + message, args);
              }
          }
      }
      Our basic plugin is done at this point! The next section will show you how to add a settings form to it

    3. speshulk926 Community Developer

      Joined:
      Dec 9, 2014
      Messages:
      911
      Likes Received:
      17
      Trophy Points:

      Simple Plugin; With Settings

      I want to preface this section with the following note. There's a great Settings window you can use instead of what is mentioned here on https://www.thebuddyforum.com/honor...4k-resolution-skinned-form-plugin-sample.html from hackersrage. I am not including that in this to keep it as simple as possible.

      We are going to start this one off based on the Simple Plugin; Without Settings. All we will be doing on this one is adding a new Settings file and allowing someone to change the health percentage of when we are going to log. This is by no means the "best method" or anything like that. It's just to show how to make it work. You must find out what the best method is for you and your plugin.

      There are 3 major components here that we will be doing:
      1. Adding a Settings class
      2. Adding a Settings Form
      3. Using the Settings class in our Plugin



      1. Add Settings class

      Right click on your project and click on Add > Class... and then name it like TBPSettings (The Best Plugin), or MySettings or something other than "Settings" and I'll show you why here.





      You want to inherit from Styx.Helpers.Settings.

      This will cause an error because the base class requires a settings path to pull from. If you want character specific settings, just add the following (I've added the full class here to show it all):

      PHP:
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TBPSettings : Styx.Helpers.Settings
                {
              public       TBPSettings()
                  :      base(System.IO.Path.Combine(CharacterSettingsDirectory, "TheBestPlugin.xml"))
              {

              }
          }
      }
      You will then want to create an Instance property so that it will either use the already-created instance of TBPSettings or create a new instance if it does not exist:

      PHP:
      using Styx.Helpers;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TBPSettings : Styx.Helpers.Settings
                {
              private static       TBPSettings _instance;

              public       TBPSettings()
                  :       base(System.IO.Path.Combine(CharacterSettingsDirectory, "TheBestPlugin.xml")) // will be '\Honorbuddy\Settings\{Server}\{CharacterName}\TheBestPlugin.xml' 
                    {

              }
              public static       TBPSettings Instance
                    {
                        get { return _instance ?? (_instance = new TBPSettings()); }
              }
          }
      }
      At this point, you have code that will compile, but no settings have been added yet.

      Add a new public int property called HealthPercentage

      Add a [Settings] attribute to it. This will cause it to error (does not know what Settings is), so use your shortcut and press Ctrl + Period and add the using statement of Styx.Helpers.

      Now you want a default value as well, which we will set at 50.


      The end result of this class is the following:

      PHP:
      using Styx.Helpers;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TBPSettings : Styx.Helpers.Settings
                {
              private static       TBPSettings _instance;

              public       TBPSettings()
                  :      base(System.IO.Path.Combine(CharacterSettingsDirectory, "TheBestPlugin.xml"))
              {

              }

              public static       TBPSettings Instance
                    {
                        get { return _instance ?? (_instance = new TBPSettings()); }
              }

              [      Setting, DefaultValue(50)]
              public       int HealthPercentage { get; set; }
          }
      }

      [HR][/HR]
      2. Adding a Settings form

      Right click on your project and click on Add > Windows Form... and name it something like SettingsForm




      Add a Label and a NumericUpDown control and name the NumericUpDown something useful like HealthPercentageAmount




      After naming the control, double click it to add a ValueChanged event handler. We now want to access our static property Settings from TheBestPlugin.cs and change the value.

      Also, you want to load the value on Initialization, so after InitializeComponent(), set the value.

      PHP:
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.ComponentModel;
      using System.Data;
      using System.Drawing;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;
      using System.Windows.Forms;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public       partial class SettingsForm : Form
                {
              public       SettingsForm()
              {
                        InitializeComponent();
                        HealthPercentageAmount.Value = TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage;
              }

              private       void HealthPercentageAmount_ValueChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
              {
                        TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage = (int)HealthPercentageAmount.Value;
              }
          }
      }
      Now we need to call this form from the previously mentioned OnButtonPressed method call on our TheBestPlugin.cs file.

      [HR][/HR]
      3. Using the Settings class in our Plugin


      Add this method (and code) to show the form and then once the form is closed, to save the file.

      PHP:
      public override void OnButtonPress()
      {
                SettingsForm frm = new SettingsForm();
                frm.ShowDialog();
                Settings.Save();
      }


      Now Add text to the button
      PHP:
      public override string ButtonText { get { return "TBP Settings..."; } }

      Now make HB know that you want to show the button
      PHP:
      public override bool WantButton { get { return true; } }

      Now we can change our hard-coded 50 to the soft-coded one from our settings file.

      PHP:
      public override void Pulse()
      {
                // if any of this is true, then we won't run the code 
                if (!Styx.StyxWoW.IsInGame || !Styx.StyxWoW.IsInWorld || Me == null || !Me.IsValid || !Me.IsAlive || Me.Mounted || !Me.Combat)
          {
              return;
          }

          if (      Me.HealthPercent < TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage)
          {
                    infoLog("Current Health dropped below {0}%! Health currently at {1}", TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage, Me.HealthPercent);
          }

      }


      This portion of the Guide is complete. Below is a full reference of every file:


      TBPSettings.cs

      PHP:
      using Styx.Helpers;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TBPSettings : Styx.Helpers.Settings
                {
              private static       TBPSettings _instance;

              public       TBPSettings()
                  :       base(System.IO.Path.Combine(CharacterSettingsDirectory, "TheBestPlugin.xml")) // will be '\Honorbuddy\Settings\{Server}\{CharacterName}\TheBestPlugin.xml' 
                    {

              }
              public static       TBPSettings Instance
                    {
                        get { return _instance ?? (_instance = new TBPSettings()); }
              }
              [      Setting, DefaultValue(50)]
              public       int HealthPercentage { get; set; }
          }
      }


      SettingsForm.cs
      PHP:
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.ComponentModel;
      using System.Data;
      using System.Drawing;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;
      using System.Windows.Forms;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public       partial class SettingsForm : Form
                {
              public       SettingsForm()
              {
                        InitializeComponent();
                        HealthPercentageAmount.Value = TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage;
              }

              private       void HealthPercentageAmount_ValueChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
              {
                        TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage = (int)HealthPercentageAmount.Value;
              }
          }
      }

      TheBestPlugin.cs

      PHP:
      using Styx.Common;
      using Styx.Plugins;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TheBestPlugin : HBPlugin
                {
              
              public       override string Author { get { return "SpeshulK926"; } }
              public       override string Name { get { return "The Best Plugin!"; } }
              public       override Version Version { get { return new Version(1, 0); } }
              public       override string ButtonText { get { return "TBP Settings..."; } }
              public       override bool WantButton { get { return true; } }

              public       Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.LocalPlayer Me { get { return Styx.StyxWoW.Me; } }

              public       override void Pulse()
              {
                        // if any of this is true, then we won't run the code  
                        if (!Styx.StyxWoW.IsInGame || !Styx.StyxWoW.IsInWorld || Me == null || !Me.IsValid || !Me.IsAlive || Me.Mounted || !Me.Combat)
                  {
                      return;
                  }

                  if (      Me.HealthPercent < TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage)
                  {
                            infoLog("Current Health dropped below {0}%! Health currently at {1}", TBPSettings.Instance.HealthPercentage, Me.HealthPercent);
                  }

              }

              public       override void OnEnable()
              {
                        base.OnEnable();
                        infoLog("Enabled!");
              }
              public       override void OnDisable()
              {
                        base.OnDisable();
                        infoLog("Disabled!");
              }

              public static       void infoLog(string message, params object[] args)
              {
                        Logging.Write(System.Windows.Media.Colors.White, "[TBP] " + message, args);
              }
              public static       void debugLog(string message, params object[] args)
              {
                        Logging.WriteQuiet(message, args);
              }

              public       override void OnButtonPress()
              {
                        SettingsForm frm = new SettingsForm();
                        frm.ShowDialog();
                        TBPSettings.Instance.Save();
              }
          }
      }
      [HR][/HR]

      Simple Plugin; With Settings - Alternative Form

      If you do not want a "Custom Look" and are just trying to get settings down on a page as quickly as possible with no mucking about, then this is how you set up a form with a property grid similar to how Singular (and many other HB settings) are done.

      We will start off with exactly what we have above, but will be adding a new Form (instead of SettingsForm) and then a few more properties to show what it can do.

      Right click on your project and click on Add > Windows Form... and name it something like SettingsFormGrid.

      Find PropertyGrid in the Toolbox and add it to your form.




      Change the Dock property on the newly added PropertyGrid to FIll to make it fill up your form.



      Create a Form_Load event by just double clicking on the form title bar.
      PHP:
      private void SettingsFormGrid_Load(object sender, EventArgs e)
      {

      }
      Populate the PropertyGrid's SelectedObject property with your Settings instance and then Load your properties fresh.

      PHP:
      private void SettingsFormGrid_Load(object sender, EventArgs e)
      {
                propertyGrid1.SelectedObject = TBPSettings.Instance;
                TBPSettings.Instance.Load();
      }
      Now that we have our Form set up to handle the properties, we need to tell the properties how to load inside the grid.

      We will go back to our TBPSettings.cs file and add some more metadata.

      There will end up being 5 total Attributes you want to add:
      • [Setting] - This you will need to let HB know this is a setting to use
      • [DefaultValue] - The default value of the Setting
      • [Category] - The property grid will add groups to it. This Category is what defines the groups.
      • [DisplayName] - What name will be attached to the property in the grid. Keep this fairly short for readability.
      • [Description] - This is what will display in the info box near the bottom of the grid when a user clicks on the property in the grid.

      Here's an example of what I did for our HealthPercentage property setting:

      PHP:
      [Setting, DefaultValue(50)]
      [      System.ComponentModel.Category("Health")]
      [      System.ComponentModel.DisplayName("Health Percentage")]
      [      System.ComponentModel.Description("Will do something awesome when your health percentage drops below this number")]
      public       int HealthPercentage { get; set; }
      Now when you load your property window after running HB you will see the Group "Health", you will see your property "Health Percentage" and you can change as much as you want.

      Since you have set it up to point to your Settings Instance, when you change a property it is "connected" and will change it in your Settings class. After closing the form, you already have a Save() routine added, so it will save it to the file.

    4. speshulk926 Community Developer

      Joined:
      Dec 9, 2014

      Simple Plugin; Coroutine Logic


      I got most of this information from Using coroutines for logic development and after bugging people to death and asking enough stupid questions, I figured out how to use these coroutines in plugins and not just CRs.


      We will start off like all the others and I'm just going to paste in from our opening thread:

      PHP:
      using Styx.Common;
      using Styx.Plugins;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TheBestPlugin : HBPlugin
                {
              public       override string Author { get { return "SpeshulK926"; } }
              public       override string Name { get { return "The Best Plugin!"; } }
              public       override Version Version { get { return new Version(1, 0); } }
              public       Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.LocalPlayer Me { get { return Styx.StyxWoW.Me; } }
              public       override void Pulse()
              {
                  throw new       NotImplementedException();
              }
              public       override void OnEnable()
              {
                        base.OnEnable();
              }
              public       override void OnDisable()
              {
                        base.OnDisable();
              }
          }
      }
      We will go ahead and add our infoLog and debugLog methods to log data to:

      PHP:
      public static void infoLog(string message, params object[] args)
      {
                Logging.Write(System.Windows.Media.Colors.White, "[TBP] " + message, args);
      }
      public static       void debugLog(string message, params object[] args)
      {
                Logging.WriteQuiet(message, args);
      }

      Now let's just add an infoLog for OnEnable and OnDisable so we know when it is on or off:

      PHP:
      public override void OnEnable()
      {
                base.OnEnable();
                infoLog("Enabled!");
      }
      public       override void OnDisable()
      {
                base.OnDisable();
                infoLog("Disabled!");
      }

      What we want to do now is set up the MainCoroutine() that will run all the time and has your code in it. This is the easiest setup for doing something like this. You can set up a List<Coroutine> to be run off of a Lua event or something if you wanted as well (if you dispose of them properly), but for this example, I am keeping it as simple as I can.

      First, we need to add our private variable Coroutine

      PHP:
      private Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine _coroutine;

      Next, inside the Pulse() method, we want to add the code to check to see if the coroutine exists yet or IsFinished (IsFinished should really never happen in our scenario). If it doesn't exist, go ahead and create it.

      PHP:
      if (_coroutine == null || _coroutine.IsFinished)
      {
          if (      _coroutine != null)
                    debugLog("The Best Plugin Ended.  Restarting...");

                _coroutine = new Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine(async () => await MainCoroutine());
      }

      try
      {
                _coroutine.Resume();
      }

      catch (      Exception ex)
      {
                debugLog(ex.Message);
      }

      Once you add that code, you will see it error out because it doesn't know what MainCoroutine() is. We can add the signature for it now and add the code inside in the next step.

      PHP:
      private async Task MainCoroutine()
      {
          while (      true)
          {
                    await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Yield();
          }
      }
      So right now all this will do is loop through and not do anything. We are going to add code to eat Goldfish when below 50% hp and then wait for 10 seconds to see if it clears up my health issues.

      In order to do that, we will need a generic method that will use an item from your bags (if available) and Log it to the window:

      PHP:
      bool UseItem(Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.WoWItem item, bool reqs)
      {
                // these were split up to make it easier to read.  You can put them together if you decide to 

                if (!reqs) { return false; }                // if it didn't meet the requirements submitted, don't use 
                if (item == null) { return false; }         // if the item is null, don't use 
                if (!item.Usable) { return false; }         // if the item is not usable, don't use 
                if (item.Cooldown > 0) { return false; }    // if it is still on cooldown, don't use 
                if (Styx.CommonBot.Frames.MerchantFrame.Instance.IsVisible) { return false; } // if you have a merchant frame up, don't use

                infoLog(string.Format($"/use {item.Name}"));  // log you are about to use it 
                if (item.Use())
          {
              return       true;
          }
          return       false;
      }

      Let's go ahead and add our method to eat Goldfish and then I'll break it down.

      PHP:
      private async Task EatSomeGoldfish()
      {
                // wait for our GCD to use the Goldfish
                await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);

                int BigFountainGoldfish = 138967; // replace with whatever you have in your bags 
                var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == BigFountainGoldfish);

                // use the Goldfish if my Health < 50%
                UseItem(item, Me.HealthPercent < 50);

                // wait 10 seconds to see if it clears up
                await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(10000);

      }
      Breaking it down:

      Code:
      await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
      This will make the Coroutine wait until your GCD is up before it continues


      Code:
      int BigFountainGoldfish = 138967; // replace with whatever you have in your bags
      This is just the ID of the item we are using that I looked up from Wowhead. You can replace it with anything you want to use.


      Code:
      var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == BigFountainGoldfish);
      This does a lookup in your bags to grab the Item that matches the ID specified.


      Code:
      UseItem(item, Me.HealthPercent < 50);
      This will call our UseItem() method and use it if your Health Percentage is < 50.


      Code:
      await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(10000);
      This will wait 10 more seconds to continue running the method.



      Now, we need to add EatSomeGoldfish() to the MainCoroutine.

      PHP:
      private async Task MainCoroutine()
      {
          while (      true)
          {
                    await EatSomeGoldfish();
                    await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Yield();
          }
      }

      At this point, we can save it and run it and it will execute our EatSomeGoldfish if your HP is < 50%. To show what you can do, let's add another method called MakeSomeoneSparkle() where the idea is to use Stardust - Item - World of Warcraft on anyone who does not already have this awesome buff!

      We will start the same way and add a method for MakeSomeoneSparkle()

      PHP:
      private async Task MakeSomeoneSparkle()
      {
                await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
                int Stardust = 52490;
                int StardustAura = 73619;

          var       item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == Stardust);
          
                // use the stardust if current target does not have the aura already 
                UseItem(item, Me.GotTarget && !Me.CurrentTarget.HasAura(StardustAura));  
      }
      Since this can be used a lot, we won't add the 10 second wait time to it.


      Now you can add it into MainCoroutine() just like the other one.

      PHP:
      private async Task MainCoroutine()
      {
          while (      true)
          {
                    await EatSomeGoldfish();
                    await MakeSomeoneSparkle();
                    await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Yield();
          }
      }
      This is the full code after we are done:

      PHP:
      using Styx.Common;
      using Styx.Plugins;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       TheBestPlugin
      {
          public class       TheBestPlugin : HBPlugin
                {
              private       Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine _coroutine;

              public       override string Author { get { return "SpeshulK926"; } }
              public       override string Name { get { return "The Best Plugin!"; } }
              public       override Version Version { get { return new Version(1, 0); } }
              public       Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.LocalPlayer Me { get { return Styx.StyxWoW.Me; } }
              public       override void OnEnable()
              {
                        base.OnEnable();
                        infoLog("Enabled!");
              }
              public       override void OnDisable()
              {
                        base.OnDisable();
                        infoLog("Disabled!");
              }

              public static       void infoLog(string message, params object[] args)
              {
                        Logging.Write(System.Windows.Media.Colors.White, "[TBP] " + message, args);
              }
              public static       void debugLog(string message, params object[] args)
              {
                        Logging.WriteQuiet(message, args);
              }

              public       override void Pulse()
              {
                  if (      _coroutine == null || _coroutine.IsFinished)
                  {
                      if (      _coroutine != null)
                                debugLog("The Best Plugin Ended.  Restarting...");

                            _coroutine = new Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine(async () => await MainCoroutine());
                  }

                  try
                  {
                            _coroutine.Resume();
                  }

                  catch (      Exception ex)
                  {
                            debugLog(ex.Message);
                  }
              }

              private       async Task MainCoroutine()
              {
                  while (      true)
                  {
                            await EatSomeGoldfish();
                            await MakeSomeoneSparkle();
                            await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Yield();
                  }
              }
              private       async Task EatSomeGoldfish()
              {
                        // wait for our GCD to use the Goldfish
                        await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);

                        int BigFountainGoldfish = 138967; // replace with whatever you have in your bags 
                        var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == BigFountainGoldfish);

                        // use the Goldfish if my Health < 50%
                        UseItem(item, Me.HealthPercent < 50);

                        // wait 10 seconds to see if it clears up
                        await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(10000);

              }

              private       async Task MakeSomeoneSparkle()
              {
                        await Buddy.Coroutines.Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
                        int Stardust = 52490;
                        int StardustAura = 73619;

                  var       item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == Stardust);
                  
                        // use the stardust if current target does not have the aura already 
                        UseItem(item, Me.GotTarget && !Me.CurrentTarget.HasAura(StardustAura));  
              }

                    bool UseItem(Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.WoWItem item, bool reqs)
              {
                        // these were split up to make it easier to read.  You can put them together if you decide to 

                        if (!reqs) { return false; }                // if it didn't meet the requirements submitted, don't use 
                        if (item == null) { return false; }         // if the item is null, don't use 
                        if (!item.Usable) { return false; }         // if the item is not usable, don't use 
                        if (item.Cooldown > 0) { return false; }    // if it is still on cooldown, don't use 
                        if (Styx.CommonBot.Frames.MerchantFrame.Instance.IsVisible) { return false; } // if you have a merchant frame up, don't use 

                        infoLog(string.Format($"/use {item.Name}"));  // log you are about to use it 
                        if (item.Use())
                  {
                      return       true;
                  }
                  return       false;
              }
          }
      }
      [HR][/HR]

      I ran this in game -- Remember to disable eating in Singular or it will just eat with Singular and this is what I got:

      The Path Distances error was because I was leaping off of a ledge to make it go down because I'm Vengeance and nothing kills me.

      Awesome Support Requests in 3 easy steps:
      1.) If you don't attach the log to your support request, I will more than likely ask you for one, so for faster help: [How to attach your log.]
      2.) Ask yourself what I would need in order to replicate your problem quickly? If you want fast support, make it easy for me to find and reproduce your issue. Please write more than "this don't work".
      3.) Please don't paste partial logs to the thread. You can't possibly know all the things I'll need to check to correctly find the bug, so your post becomes clutter that I have to ask a mod to remove.
      ***** Did you love one of my profiles, and want to buy me a coffee so I can make more?: [Send a donation using paypal!]

      Maelnum said: ↑
      Hey, thank you very much for writing this guide.

      Looking forward to learning a bit about tree hooks and difference vs. using MainCoroutine like described above. I made a plugin that loots treasures found in player's proximity and copied its logic from another plugin which used tree hooks. It works, but I have no idea why .
      Click to expand...
      Well typically Treehooks are for replacing or adding onto different routines. For instance, you may want to replace how you loot in certain conditions. You add the treehook for looting and it'll either add or replace the logic depending on how you write it. When I get to the article, it'll be regarding adding stuff to combat routines since I did a plugin for using follower abilities using the treehook already. I'll get to it again soon!

      Yeah, sorry that one was untested. I just wrote out the code while HB was down. I believe if you change the awaits in EatSomeGoldfish and MakeSomeoneSparkle from await Coroutine.Yield() at the end to await Task.Delay(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft); at the beginning it will fix it.

      Example:
      Code:
      private async Task MakeSomeoneSparkle()
        {
            await Task.Delay(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
            int Stardust = 52490;
            int StardustAura = 73619;

            var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == Stardust);
            UseItem(item, Me.GotTarget && !Me.CurrentTarget.HasAura(StardustAura));  // use the stardust if current target doe snot have the aura already
        }

    15. MaiN Moderator Staff Member Moderator Buddy Core Dev

      Joined:
      Jan 15, 2010
      Messages:
      1,017
      Likes Received:
      35
      Trophy Points:
      48
      speshulk926 said: ↑
      Yeah, sorry that one was untested. I just wrote out the code while HB was down. I believe if you change the awaits in EatSomeGoldfish and MakeSomeoneSparkle from await Coroutine.Yield() at the end to await Task.Delay(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft); at the beginning it will fix it.

      Example:
      Code:
      private async Task MakeSomeoneSparkle()
        {
            await Task.Delay(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
            int Stardust = 52490;
            int StardustAura = 73619;

            var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == Stardust);
            UseItem(item, Me.GotTarget && !Me.CurrentTarget.HasAura(StardustAura));  // use the stardust if current target doe snot have the aura already
        }
      Click to expand...
      External tasks should not be used from coroutines. Read my guidelines here: https://www.thebuddyforum.com/honor...62972-using-coroutines-logic-development.html
      You can automatically catch errors like these by installing the NuGet package Buddy.Analyzers to your project.

      EDIT: I looked at your code. Those tasks (EatSomeGoldfish, MakeSomeoneSparkle) should not be external tasks. You are causing a transition onto another thread by using Task.Delay, which will cause threading issues.
       
      Last edited: Oct 6, 2016

    16. MaiN Moderator Staff Member Moderator Buddy Core Dev

      Joined:

      It should look something like:
      Code:
      using Buddy.Coroutines;
using Styx.Common; 
using Styx.Plugins; 
using System; 
using System.Collections.Generic; 
using System.Linq; 
using System.Text; 
using System.Threading.Tasks; 

namespace TheBestPlugin 
{ 
    public class TheBestPlugin : HBPlugin 
    { 
        private static bool _enabled = false; 
        public override string Author { get { return "SpeshulK926"; } } 
        public override string Name { get { return "The Best Plugin!"; } } 
        public override Version Version { get { return new Version(1, 0); } } 
        public Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.LocalPlayer Me { get { return Styx.StyxWoW.Me; } } 
        private Coroutine _coroutine; 
        public override void Pulse() 
        { 
            if (_enabled) 
            { 
                // if any of this is true, then we won't run the code  
                if (!Styx.StyxWoW.IsInGame || !Styx.StyxWoW.IsInWorld || Me == null || !Me.IsValid || !Me.IsAlive || Me.Mounted || !Me.Combat) 
                { 
                    return; 
                } 

                if (_coroutine == null || _coroutine.IsFinished) 
                { 
                    if (_coroutine != null) 
                        debugLog("The Best Plugin Ended.  Restarting..."); 

                    _coroutine = Coroutine(async () => await MainCoroutine()); 
                } 

                try 
                { 
                    _coroutine.Resume(); 
                } 

                catch (Exception ex) 
                { 
                    debugLog(ex.Message); 
                } 
            } 
        } 

        private async Task<bool> MainCoroutine() 
        { 
            while (true) 
            { 
                await EatSomeGoldfish(); 
                await MakeSomeoneSparkle(); 
				await Coroutine.Yield();
            } 

            return true; 
        } 

        private async Task EatSomeGoldfish() 
        { 
            await Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
            int BigFountainGoldfish = 138967; // replace with whatever you have in your bags 
            var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == BigFountainGoldfish); 
            UseItem(item, Me.HealthPercent < 50); 

        } 

        private async Task MakeSomeoneSparkle() 
        { 
            await Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft); 
            int Stardust = 52490; 
            int StardustAura = 73619; 

            var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == Stardust); 
            UseItem(item, Me.GotTarget && !Me.CurrentTarget.HasAura(StardustAura));  // use the stardust if current target doe snot have the aura already 
        } 

        bool UseItem(Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.WoWItem item, bool reqs) 
        { 
            // these were split up to make it easier to read.  You can put them together if you decide to 

            if (!reqs) { return false; }                // if it didn't meet the requirements submitted, don't use 
            if (item == null) { return false; }         // if the item is null, don't use 
            if (!item.Usable) { return false; }         // if the item is not usable, don't use 
            if (item.Cooldown > 0) { return false; }    // if it is still on cooldown, don't use 
            if (Styx.CommonBot.Frames.MerchantFrame.Instance.IsVisible) { return false; } // if you have a merchant frame up, don't use 

            infoLog(string.Format($"/use {item.Name}"));  // log you are about to use it 
            if (item.Use()) 
            { 
                return true; 
            } 
            return false; 
        } 

        private static bool _initialized = false; 
        public override void OnEnable() 
        { 
            if (!_initialized) 
            { 
                base.OnEnable(); 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotStopped += OnBotStopped; 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotPaused += OnBotPaused; 
                _enabled = true; 
                _initialized = true; 
                infoLog("Enabled!"); 
            } 
        } 

        private void OnBotPaused(object sender, EventArgs e) 
        { 
            StopCoroutine(); 
        } 

        private void OnBotStopped(EventArgs args) 
        { 
            StopCoroutine(); 
        } 

        public override void OnDisable() 
        { 
            base.OnDisable(); 
            if (_initialized) 
            { 
                _enabled = false; 
                infoLog("Disabled!"); 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotStopped -= OnBotStopped; 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotPaused -= OnBotPaused; 
                _initialized = false; 
            }
			
			StopCoroutine();
        } 

        private void StopCoroutine() 
        { 
            if (_coroutine == null) 
                return; 

            _coroutine.Dispose(); 
            _coroutine = null; 

        } 

        public static void infoLog(string message, params object[] args) 
        { 
            Logging.Write(System.Windows.Media.Colors.White, "[TBP] " + message, args); 
        } 
        public static void debugLog(string message, params object[] args) 
        { 
            Logging.WriteQuiet(message, args); 
        } 
    } 
}
      You can also get rid of _enabled. Your plugin will not be pulsed if it isn't enabled.
      _initialized is also unnecessary.
       
      Hawker says (14:44):
      and uts been werl ovber ayear sinc eI wa sthere
      Click to expand...
      MaiN, Oct 6, 2016 Report

    17. speshulk926 Community Developer

      Joined:
      Dec 9, 2014
      Messages:
      911
      Likes Received:
      17
      Trophy Points:
      18
      MaiN said: ↑
      External tasks should not be used from coroutines. Read my guidelines here: https://www.thebuddyforum.com/honor...62972-using-coroutines-logic-development.html
      You can automatically catch errors like these by installing the NuGet package Buddy.Analyzers to your project.

      EDIT: I looked at your code. Those tasks (EatSomeGoldfish, MakeSomeoneSparkle) should not be external tasks. You are causing a transition onto another thread by using Task.Delay, which will cause threading issues.
      Click to expand...
      Thank you for the response. I definitely do not have everything correct here which is why I had a giant "Untested Code" part at the start of those. I will go ahead and download the Analyzers and check it out. Having said that, I grabbed some of this code from https://www.thebuddyforum.com/honorbuddy-forum/plugins/leveling/269383-plugin-cleanmybags-beta.html which appears to do that. Is that one wrong as well? If not, what am I doing different? Snippet found here so you don't have to find it:

      PHP:
      private async Task MainCoroutine()
      {
          while (!      _quit)
          {
              try
              {
                        Logging.WriteDiagnostic(Colors.Blue, "Running Logic @ " + DateTime.Now);
                  var       items = StyxWoW.Me.BagItems;
                        //Check for simple container items
                        await Coroutine.ExternalTask(simpleContainerItems(items));
                        //Check and open any container Items
                        await Coroutine.ExternalTask(ContainerItems);
                        //Check if we have Artifact items to use
                        await Coroutine.ExternalTask(ArtifactPower);
                        //Check if we have Ancient Mana items to use
                        await Coroutine.ExternalTask(AncientMana);

                        //if we looted sort our bags
                        if (_looted)
                            SortBags();

                        await Coroutine.Sleep(10000);

                        await Coroutine.Yield();
              }
              catch (      Exception ex)
              {
                        Logging.WriteDiagnostic(ex.ToString());
              }
          }
      }
      PHP:
      private async Task ContainerItems()
      {
                await Task.Delay(SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
          try
          {
              foreach (var       i in StyxWoW.Me.BagItems.FindAll(x => x.Effects.Count > 0 && x.Effects[0].Spell.SpellEffects[0].EffectType == WoWSpellEffectType.CreateRandomItem))
              {
                  var       test = ItemEffectList.FromItemInfo(i.ItemInfo);
                  foreach (var       e in test)
                  {
                            Logging.Write(string.Format("Opening {0}", i.SafeName));
                            Logging.Write("Opening container item" + i.SafeName);
                            i.Use();
                  }
              }
          }

          catch (      Exception ex)
          {

          }
      }

      MaiN said: ↑
      It should look something like:
      Code:
      using Buddy.Coroutines;
using Styx.Common; 
using Styx.Plugins; 
using System; 
using System.Collections.Generic; 
using System.Linq; 
using System.Text; 
using System.Threading.Tasks; 

namespace TheBestPlugin 
{ 
    public class TheBestPlugin : HBPlugin 
    { 
        private static bool _enabled = false; 
        public override string Author { get { return "SpeshulK926"; } } 
        public override string Name { get { return "The Best Plugin!"; } } 
        public override Version Version { get { return new Version(1, 0); } } 
        public Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.LocalPlayer Me { get { return Styx.StyxWoW.Me; } } 
        private Coroutine _coroutine; 
        public override void Pulse() 
        { 
            if (_enabled) 
            { 
                // if any of this is true, then we won't run the code  
                if (!Styx.StyxWoW.IsInGame || !Styx.StyxWoW.IsInWorld || Me == null || !Me.IsValid || !Me.IsAlive || Me.Mounted || !Me.Combat) 
                { 
                    return; 
                } 

                if (_coroutine == null || _coroutine.IsFinished) 
                { 
                    if (_coroutine != null) 
                        debugLog("The Best Plugin Ended.  Restarting..."); 

                    _coroutine = Coroutine(async () => await MainCoroutine()); 
                } 

                try 
                { 
                    _coroutine.Resume(); 
                } 

                catch (Exception ex) 
                { 
                    debugLog(ex.Message); 
                } 
            } 
        } 

        private async Task<bool> MainCoroutine() 
        { 
            while (true) 
            { 
                await EatSomeGoldfish(); 
                await MakeSomeoneSparkle(); 
				await Coroutine.Yield();
            } 

            return true; 
        } 

        private async Task EatSomeGoldfish() 
        { 
            await Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft);
            int BigFountainGoldfish = 138967; // replace with whatever you have in your bags 
            var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == BigFountainGoldfish); 
            UseItem(item, Me.HealthPercent < 50); 

        } 

        private async Task MakeSomeoneSparkle() 
        { 
            await Coroutine.Sleep(Styx.CommonBot.SpellManager.GlobalCooldownLeft); 
            int Stardust = 52490; 
            int StardustAura = 73619; 

            var item = Me.BagItems.FirstOrDefault(i => i.ItemInfo.Id == Stardust); 
            UseItem(item, Me.GotTarget && !Me.CurrentTarget.HasAura(StardustAura));  // use the stardust if current target doe snot have the aura already 
        } 

        bool UseItem(Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects.WoWItem item, bool reqs) 
        { 
            // these were split up to make it easier to read.  You can put them together if you decide to 

            if (!reqs) { return false; }                // if it didn't meet the requirements submitted, don't use 
            if (item == null) { return false; }         // if the item is null, don't use 
            if (!item.Usable) { return false; }         // if the item is not usable, don't use 
            if (item.Cooldown > 0) { return false; }    // if it is still on cooldown, don't use 
            if (Styx.CommonBot.Frames.MerchantFrame.Instance.IsVisible) { return false; } // if you have a merchant frame up, don't use 

            infoLog(string.Format($"/use {item.Name}"));  // log you are about to use it 
            if (item.Use()) 
            { 
                return true; 
            } 
            return false; 
        } 

        private static bool _initialized = false; 
        public override void OnEnable() 
        { 
            if (!_initialized) 
            { 
                base.OnEnable(); 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotStopped += OnBotStopped; 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotPaused += OnBotPaused; 
                _enabled = true; 
                _initialized = true; 
                infoLog("Enabled!"); 
            } 
        } 

        private void OnBotPaused(object sender, EventArgs e) 
        { 
            StopCoroutine(); 
        } 

        private void OnBotStopped(EventArgs args) 
        { 
            StopCoroutine(); 
        } 

        public override void OnDisable() 
        { 
            base.OnDisable(); 
            if (_initialized) 
            { 
                _enabled = false; 
                infoLog("Disabled!"); 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotStopped -= OnBotStopped; 
                Styx.CommonBot.BotEvents.OnBotPaused -= OnBotPaused; 
                _initialized = false; 
            }
			
			StopCoroutine();
        } 

        private void StopCoroutine() 
        { 
            if (_coroutine == null) 
                return; 

            _coroutine.Dispose(); 
            _coroutine = null; 

        } 

        public static void infoLog(string message, params object[] args) 
        { 
            Logging.Write(System.Windows.Media.Colors.White, "[TBP] " + message, args); 
        } 
        public static void debugLog(string message, params object[] args) 
        { 
            Logging.WriteQuiet(message, args); 
        } 
    } 
}

      _initialized is also unnecessary.
